title: ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects
summary: The Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR), India , in partnership with the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), Japan , has officially announced the Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027 . This prestigious bilateral research funding initiative aims to strengthen academic cooperation between India and Japan by supporting high-quality collaborative research projects and joint seminars in the fields of Social Sciences and Humanities . The program is designed to build sustainable international research networks, promote knowledge exchange between leading scholars, encourage the participation of early-career researchers, and address pressing social and economic challenges through collaborative research. Successful Indian research teams can receive funding of up to ₹14,00,000 over two years for Joint Research Projects or up to ₹7,00,000 for Joint Seminars. The application deadline for Indian applicants is 3 September 2026 . About the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Research Program is a bilateral academic cooperation initiative established under an agreement between the Indian Council of Social Science Research and the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science. The initiative seeks to foster long-term collaboration between researchers from India and Japan through: Joint Research Projects Joint Academic Seminars Research networking Exchange of expertise Development of young researchers International academic partnerships Rather than supporting isolated research activities, the program focuses on building sustainable collaborations capable of generating long-term academic and societal impact. Objectives of the Program The program has several important objectives, including: Promoting collaborative research between Indian and Japanese scholars. Strengthening academic cooperation between universities and research institutions. Supporting innovative social science and humanities research. Encouraging interdisciplinary approaches to solving social problems. Facilitating international exchange of knowledge and research expertise. Developing long-term international research networks. Supporting the participation and professional growth of young researchers. Producing research that contributes to policy development and socioeconomic advancement. Types of Research Activities Supported The FY2027 call supports two categories of collaborative activities. 1. Joint Research Projects These projects involve collaborative research conducted by teams from India and Japan. Key features include: Duration of two years Maximum of 3 projects expected to be funded Projects begin on 1 April 2027 Funding of up to ₹7,00,000 per fiscal year Maximum total funding of ₹14,00,000 per project The research must fall within: Social Sciences Humanities 2. Joint Seminars The program also funds collaborative seminars involving researchers from India and Japan. Features include: Duration of up to one week Maximum of 2 seminars Seminars must take place between 1 April 2027 and 31 March 2028 Funding up to ₹7,00,000 per seminar Seminars may be hosted in either: India Japan Research Fields The call is specifically intended for research in: Social Sciences Humanities Researchers working across interdisciplinary themes within these fields are encouraged to apply. Who Can Apply? Indian applicants must satisfy several eligibility requirements. Applicants must: Hold a permanent research or academic position. Be employed at a recognized institution. Hold a PhD or equivalent qualification. Demonstrate strong research capability. Have an established publication record or academic achievements. Be interested in conducting high-quality collaborative international research. Eligible Indian institutions include: Publicly funded universities Publicly funded colleges UGC-recognized deemed universities Public research institutes ICSSR research institutes ICSSR-recognized research institutions Visit HERE to learn more about the ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027. Eligibility for Japanese Researchers Japanese participants must belong to: Universities Research institutes operating under the jurisdiction of the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS). Participation Rules For Joint Research Projects: No limit on the number of participants. Researchers from third countries are not permitted . For Joint Seminars: No restriction on participant numbers. Researchers from third countries may participate only if they cover their own expenses. Funding Available Joint Research Projects ICSSR provides financial support for Indian research teams. Supported expenses include: International Travel Airfare between India and Japan Maintenance allowances are provided by JSPS. Domestic Travel Support includes: Travel expenses for Indian researchers Domestic travel costs for Japanese researchers while in India Maintenance allowances for Japanese researchers in India Other Research Costs Funding may cover: Consumables Printing Communication Honoraria Meetings Administrative research expenses ICSSR recommends that more than 50% of the project budget be allocated toward international and domestic travel. Joint Seminars in India Funding includes: Domestic travel Meeting expenses Communication Printing Reception Excursions Honoraria Seminar logistics Japanese researchers domestic expenses and maintenance allowances are covered during their stay in India. Joint Seminars in Japan ICSSR funds: International airfare Domestic travel within India for preparation meetings Communication expenses Printing Preparatory meetings Seminar organization costs in Japan are covered by the Japanese counterpart. Application Process Indian researchers must submit their applications to ICSSR. Japanese researchers must separately submit matching applications to JSPS. Applications submitted by only one side will not be considered . Applicants should therefore coordinate carefully with their international research partners before submission. APPLY HERE Application Requirements Applicants should complete the prescribed application forms provided by ICSSR. Required documents include: Completed application form Research proposal Institutional affiliation details Supporting documents required under ICSSR guidelines Researchers previously funded under ICSSR international programs during the past five years must also explain: Results achieved Expected outcomes Relevance to the new proposal Selection Criteria Applications undergo rigorous evaluation. Assessment criteria include: Scientific excellence. Quality of research proposal. Importance of international collaboration. Knowledge transfer potential. Contribution to solving social challenges. Socioeconomic impact. Participation of young researchers. Feasibility of implementation. Research methodology. Policy relevance. Budget appropriateness. Suitability of seminar venue (where applicable). Review committees from ICSSR and JSPS may conduct separate evaluations before jointly selecting successful proposals. Obligations of Selected Researchers Successful Indian team leaders must: Submit implementation plans. Request project funding using prescribed forms. Administer funds through their affiliated institutions. Submit final reports. Publicly acknowledge ICSSR and JSPS support in publications. Complete research ethics education (for research projects). Ethical Responsibilities ICSSR maintains strict standards regarding research integrity. Funding may be terminated if researchers engage in: Fabrication Falsification Plagiarism Misuse of funds Harassment Discrimination Violations of applicable laws Researchers are expected to maintain the highest ethical standards throughout the project. Intellectual Property Project leaders from both countries must ensure: Proper protection of intellectual property Fair distribution of research outputs Compliance with national regulations Institutions are encouraged to establish appropriate intellectual property policies before the project begins. Important Notes Applicants should keep the following in mind: Matching applications are mandatory from both India and Japan. Projects start from 1 April 2027 . Selection numbers may change depending on available budgets. ICSSR does not arrange visas or accommodation. ICSSR is not responsible for accidents or medical emergencies during project-related travel. Reports submitted may be published on the ICSSR website. Application Deadline Deadline for Indian researchers: 3 September 2026 Late applications are not expected to be accepted. Applicants are encouraged to coordinate with their Japanese counterparts well in advance to ensure both submissions are completed before the deadline. Conclusion The ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity for experienced researchers in India and Japan to establish meaningful international collaborations in the social sciences and humanities. Beyond financial support, the initiative promotes long-term academic partnerships, interdisciplinary research, knowledge exchange, and the development of early-career researchers. Institutions and scholars seeking to address important societal challenges through collaborative research should take advantage of this prestigious bilateral funding opportunity by preparing strong proposals and ensuring coordinated submissions from both partner countries before the application deadline of 3 September 2026 . For more global grant opportunity visit OFY HERE The post ICSSR-JSPS Joint Call for Research Proposals FY 2027: Up to ₹14 Lakh Research Funding for India-Japan Collaborative Social Science Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates
summary: The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is inviting applications for the position of Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme . This exciting part-time opportunity is designed for passionate professionals who are interested in cancer prevention, youth engagement, leadership, and international collaboration. The successful candidate will play a central role in coordinating one of Europe s leading youth advocacy initiatives focused on cancer prevention. Working closely with the ECL Secretariat and Board, the Coordinator will oversee the implementation of the programme s strategic goals while supporting young advocates from across Europe. This role offers flexible remote working arrangements, competitive hourly compensation, and the opportunity to contribute to a meaningful public health mission that has positively impacted young people from more than 30 European countries. About the Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) The Association of European Cancer Leagues (ECL) is a respected non-profit umbrella organization representing 35 national and regional cancer leagues across Europe . The organization works to improve cancer prevention, treatment, patient care, and public health policies throughout Europe. Its vision is straightforward but ambitious: A Europe free of cancer. ECL members include trusted non-profit organizations that provide: Cancer prevention education Patient support services Public awareness campaigns Advocacy for stronger cancer policies Information and healthcare resources ECL also maintains strict ethical standards by refusing financial support from the tobacco industry and other companies whose products are considered harmful to health. About the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme Launched in 2015 , the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme has become one of Europe s leading youth-led cancer prevention initiatives. The programme brings together enthusiastic young people committed to improving public health through awareness, education, advocacy, and policy engagement. Its primary educational framework is the European Code Against Cancer , which serves as a practical guide for reducing cancer risks through healthy lifestyle choices and preventive measures. Since its establishment, the programme has successfully built a network of youth advocates from more than 30 European countries . Participants come from diverse academic and professional backgrounds including: Medicine Public Health Political Science Law Health Policy Social Sciences Research Other health-related disciplines Throughout the year, Youth Ambassadors participate in: Public awareness campaigns International events Educational workshops Online advocacy initiatives Summer Schools Networking activities Policy discussions Community engagement projects The programme empowers young leaders to promote cancer prevention at both national and European Union levels. About the Coordinator Position ECL is seeking a part-time Youth Ambassadors Programme Coordinator who will provide operational leadership and strategic coordination for the programme. The selected individual will work closely with: The ECL Secretariat The ECL Board The Senior Policy Officer for Cancer Prevention Youth Ambassadors across Europe The Coordinator will ensure that the programme continues to grow while successfully implementing its strategic objectives and annual action plans. Although the initial contract lasts for one year , ECL intends to establish a longer-term working relationship with the successful candidate, subject to performance and future collaboration opportunities. Key Responsibilities The Coordinator will perform a wide range of leadership, planning, and administrative responsibilities. These include: 1. Programme Coordination The Coordinator will oversee the day-to-day management of the Youth Ambassadors Programme. Responsibilities include: Coordinating programme activities Supporting Youth Ambassadors Maintaining effective communication with members Facilitating collaboration among participants 2. Strategic Leadership The successful candidate will help implement the programme s long-term strategy by: Drafting Action Plans Implementing strategic objectives Monitoring programme progress Reporting on programme achievements Evaluating outcomes and future priorities 3. Funding Support The Coordinator will work with the Secretariat to identify and support funding opportunities that strengthen the programme s sustainability. Tasks may include: Researching funding opportunities Assisting with grant-related activities Supporting project development 4. Event Organisation The Coordinator will organize major programme activities including: Annual Summer School Youth Ambassador meetings Workshops Online events Additional programme activities when required. Who Should Apply? ECL is searching for an organized, motivated, and reliable individual with a passion for youth leadership and cancer prevention. Applicants should demonstrate the ability to work independently while collaborating with an international team. Priority consideration will be given to current or former members of the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme , although applications from all qualified candidates are encouraged. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should possess the following qualifications: A university degree Familiarity with the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme Strong organizational skills Excellent written and spoken English High levels of trustworthiness and reliability Experience in or strong interest in cancer prevention Ability to work in an international and multicultural environment Benefits of the Position Successful applicants will receive several professional benefits, including: One-year part-time contract Possibility of contract extension Flexible remote working arrangements Opportunity to work from Brussels, Belgium Competitive hourly rate of €30 Flexible workload of approximately 10–20 hours per month Opportunity to collaborate with leading European public health professionals Valuable international networking experience Leadership experience within a major European non-profit organization Contribution to cancer prevention initiatives across Europe Work Location Applicants may choose between: Remote work Brussels, Belgium The flexible work arrangement allows candidates from different locations to contribute effectively while maintaining work-life balance. Contract Details Contract Duration: One year Extension: Possible based on future collaboration Employment Type: Part-time freelance consultancy Expected Start Date: As soon as possible Workload: Approximately 10–20 hours per month Hourly Rate: €30 How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare the following documents: Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Two professional references Letter of motivation Applications should be submitted via email: vacancy@cancer.eu Email Subject Line: Application – YA coordinator The deadline for submitting applications is 22 July 2026 . Learn more about this job HERE Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible because: Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis . Shortlisted candidates will be contacted within one week after the application deadline. Due to the anticipated high volume of applications, ECL will not provide individual feedback to unsuccessful applicants. Why You Should Consider This Opportunity This position offers much more than a part-time consultancy role. It provides an opportunity to contribute directly to one of Europe s most impactful youth health advocacy programmes. Professionals interested in public health, cancer prevention, youth empowerment, international cooperation, and policy advocacy will gain valuable experience working with experts and young leaders from across Europe. The role also offers flexibility, international exposure, leadership experience, and the chance to make a meaningful difference in cancer prevention efforts that benefit communities across Europe. Whether you have previously participated in the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme or are an experienced professional with a passion for public health, this position provides an excellent platform for advancing your career while contributing to an important cause. Conclusion The Coordinator for the ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme is an outstanding opportunity for individuals who are passionate about youth leadership, public health, and cancer prevention. With flexible remote working options, competitive compensation, and the opportunity to coordinate an influential international programme, this role is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact within the European public health sector. Interested candidates should ensure they submit a complete application before the 22 July 2026 deadline to maximize their chances of consideration. For more Global JOBS click HERE The post Coordinator for ECL Youth Ambassadors Programme (2026): Remote Opportunity to Lead Europe’s Next Generation of Cancer Prevention Advocates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UN Innovation Training Library 2026: Explore Courses, Toolkits and Future-Ready Professional Skills
summary: The United Nations Innovation Network provides an extensive Innovation Training Library containing courses, toolkits, learning paths, professional certificates and other capacity-building resources developed or hosted by organizations across the United Nations system. The library is intended to help United Nations personnel and other interested learners build practical innovation capabilities. It allows users to explore new approaches, understand emerging technologies and consider how innovative methods can be applied within United Nations operations, public institutions, development programmes and other professional environments. Rather than operating as one course with a single application form, deadline or eligibility requirement, the UN Innovation Training Library functions as a searchable directory. Each listed resource has its own subject, duration, delivery format, intended audience and access arrangements. The library covers areas such as: Artificial intelligence and machine learning Generative artificial intelligence Data innovation and analytics Digital transformation Behavioural science Strategic foresight Human-centred design Innovation management Innovation scaling Sustainable and innovative finance Blockchain Automation and robotics Internet of Things Communications and knowledge sharing Entrepreneurship Climate action Digital health Digital agriculture Tourism innovation Urban development Aviation innovation Frontier technologies Important Clarification About the Number of Resources The training page currently displays a gallery counter showing 99 results. However, this number represents the total results appearing in the library at the time the page is viewed. It should not be interpreted as 99 conventional courses because the collection also contains: Toolkits Learning paths Professional certificate programmes Training compilations Fellowship programmes Accelerator programmes Mobile microlearning resources Other professional development materials For this reason, it is more accurate to describe the platform as a growing collection of innovation-related courses, toolkits and learning resources rather than stating that it provides a fixed number of courses. The library is also described as a living library, meaning that its content and total number of results can change when new resources are added or existing resources are revised or removed. What Is the UN Innovation Training Library? The UN Innovation Training Library is a curated collection of learning resources from across the United Nations system. Its purpose is to support the development of innovation capabilities among UN staff and other interested users. The official page states that the library can support both people who are new to innovation and professionals who are already leading teams or innovation-related activities. The library can be used by learners seeking to: Understand the fundamentals of innovation. Develop an innovation-oriented professional mindset. Learn how artificial intelligence can support organizational work. Understand the ethical and governance implications of AI. Improve data literacy, analysis and visualization. Use evidence more effectively in policymaking. Explore digital transformation in government and development. Apply behavioural science to public policy and programme design. Use human-centred design to improve products and services. Learn about sustainable finance and climate-related investment. Study emerging technologies such as blockchain and robotics. Improve training, facilitation and communication skills. Explore innovation in agriculture, health, tourism, aviation and cities. Connect professional learning with the Sustainable Development Goals. How the Training Library Is Organized The page contains filters that allow users to search according to development priorities, training providers, innovation subjects, time requirements and delivery methods. 1. Sustainable Development Goal Filters Users can search for resources related to humanitarian action or the Sustainable Development Goals. The available filters include: Humanitarian Action SDG 1: No Poverty SDG 2: Zero Hunger SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being SDG 4: Quality Education SDG 5: Gender Equality SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy SDG 8: Decent Work and Economic Growth SDG 9: Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure SDG 10: Reduced Inequalities SDG 11: Sustainable Cities and Communities SDG 12: Responsible Consumption and Production SDG 13: Climate Action SDG 14: Life Below Water SDG 15: Life on Land SDG 16: Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals These filters allow learners to connect their professional-development choices with specific development, humanitarian, environmental or governance priorities. 2. Training Provider Filters The library contains resources associated with numerous United Nations entities, specialized agencies and international institutions. Examples appearing in the provider filter include: Food and Agriculture Organization International Civil Aviation Organization International Labour Organization International Telecommunication Union United Nations Capital Development Fund United Nations Conference on Trade and Development United Nations Development Programme United Nations Environment Programme United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change United Nations Global Pulse United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees United Nations Human Settlements Programme United Nations Institute for Training and Research United Nations International Computing Centre United Nations System Staff College United Nations Tourism United Nations University UN Women UNICEF World Food Programme World Health Organization World Bank Group World Intellectual Property Organization World Meteorological Organization The complete filter contains many additional United Nations departments, offices and partner organizations. 3. Innovation Subject Filters Users can search the library according to the following innovation-related themes: 3D printing Artificial intelligence and machine learning Automation and robotics Behavioural science Blockchain Chatbots Communications and knowledge sharing Culture of innovation Data innovation Digital transformation Drones and satellite imagery Frontier technologies Generative AI Innovation in business processes Innovation in general, including human-centred design Innovation scaling Innovative finance Internet of Things Mobile phone-based innovations New business models Strategic foresight Virtual reality and augmented reality These categories demonstrate that the library addresses both technology-driven innovation and wider institutional or organizational change. 4. Time Requirements The library divides resources into four broad time categories: Short: A few hours Medium: A few days Long: A few weeks Very long: A few months The duration assigned to each listing is an indicative category rather than a detailed schedule. Users should check the individual programme page for the actual number of hours, days or weeks required. 5. Delivery Methods The available delivery modalities are: Online In-person Hybrid Most displayed resources are online, but the library also includes classroom-based and blended programmes. Major Learning Areas Available in the Library Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Artificial intelligence is one of the most prominent subject areas represented in the library. The available resources cover different levels of AI learning, including: Basic AI literacy Generative AI Large language models Prompt engineering AI governance AI ethics AI applications in health AI applications in hospitality AI for learning professionals AI for decision-makers AI and data storytelling AI skills for UN personnel AI Academy The AI Academy was developed and is operated by the UNICC AI Hub. It seeks to address the artificial-intelligence literacy gap across United Nations entities by offering a structured, multi-partner initiative aligned with UN 2.0 and the World Food Programme’s Data and AI Competency Framework. The library classifies it as a medium-duration online resource. Artificial Intelligence for Learning Professionals This UNITAR course examines how AI can be integrated into learning design without sacrificing empathy, human judgement and professional insight. It combines areas such as: Behavioural science Instructional design Strategic foresight Ethical AI Human-centred learning The programme is presented as a short online course. Artificial Intelligence Masterclass The Artificial Intelligence Masterclass explores the influence of AI on: Training Education Knowledge management Workforce development Everyday professional practices The course also examines emerging trends, ethical considerations and practical applications. Its description identifies students, educators and professionals as potential learners. AI Skills for UN Personnel AI Skills for UN Personnel is led by the United Nations System Staff College in collaboration with the Executive Office of the Secretary-General and Microsoft Elevate. It supports the UN 2.0 and UN80 agendas and is classified as a medium-duration hybrid programme. AI Essentials for Decision Makers This UNITAR programme is intended to help institutional leaders, government professionals and organizational decision-makers understand AI and lead digital transformation responsibly. It connects technical concepts with practical policy and management applications, including: Governance Strategic foresight Organizational performance Responsible digital transformation The library identifies it as a long online programme. UN Generative AI Professional Certificate The UN Generative AI Professional Certificate provides learning on the mechanisms and applications of generative AI in: Text Images Video It is listed as a long hybrid programme rather than a multi-month programme. Generative AI With Large Language Models This course provides more technical learning about the lifecycle of large language model applications. It addresses: Data gathering Model selection Transformer architecture Model training Fine-tuning Performance evaluation Deployment Scaling laws Compute requirements Inference Business opportunities and challenges It is categorized as a medium-duration online course. AI Governance, Ethics and Responsible Technology The library does not present AI only as a technical subject. Several listings focus on responsible governance and ethical use. These include: Artificial Intelligence Governance Toolkit AI Governance in Practice: Developing Secure and Innovative Frameworks Generative AI and Ethics: The Urgency of Now Ethics and Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health Introduction to Artificial Intelligence and Women, Peace and Security Emerging Technologies and Human Rights 2026 Foundations of Humane Technology These resources address issues such as: Privacy Governance Regulation Security Human rights Gender responsiveness Conflict sensitivity Healthcare ethics Stakeholder engagement Organizational responsibility The Artificial Intelligence Governance Toolkit, for example, provides a framework for addressing privacy and governance at organizational and system levels. Data Analytics, Data Literacy and Data Storytelling The library contains several programmes for professionals who need to analyse, visualize, interpret and communicate data. E-Learning Path on Data Analytics The UN System Staff College’s E-Learning Path on Data Analytics is intended to strengthen participants’ ability to: Analyse data Visualize information Prepare reports Communicate evidence Learners complete modules and activities at their own pace while participating in online discussions and interacting with faculty and peers. It is listed as a long online learning path. UN Data Analytics Professional Certificate This programme combines: Self-paced modules Instructor-led sessions On-the-job practice Data analysis Data visualization Reporting Communication It is tailored to the UN context and is categorized as a very long online programme requiring several months. Introduction to Data Literacy This self-paced course introduces basic data literacy. It covers: Data input Data cleaning Data usability Interpretation Context Data storytelling It is classified as a short online course. Kamino 100 Series The Kamino 100 Series introduces the data science lifecycle, including: Data ingestion Data preparation Data visualization Machine learning Text analysis Storytelling The listing states that it is designed for learners regardless of their previous data-science knowledge or professional background. Digital Transformation and Digital Governance Digital transformation resources examine how institutions can design, manage and implement technology-enabled systems responsibly. Digital Fitness Programme The UNDP Digital Fitness Programme is a hands-on digital product design and management training programme. It is intended for operations and programme managers seeking to develop and roll out digital solutions. The library categorizes it as a medium-duration, in-person programme. Digital Health: Planning National Systems This WHO programme focuses on strengthening national digital-health governance and workforce capacity. It addresses: National digital-health strategies Digital architecture Standards Information infrastructure Emergency-preparedness technologies National planning Implementation capacity It is classified as a short online programme. Digital Agriculture The World Bank Group’s Digital Agriculture course examines digital agricultural technologies such as: Mobile applications Digital farmer identities Solar applications Portable agricultural devices Digital marketplaces Agricultural data platforms The description states that the course lasts five weeks and includes discussion forums and quizzes. However, the library’s broad duration filter labels it as medium, illustrating why users should check the detailed description rather than relying only on the general duration category. Digital4Sustainability Learning Path This learning path examines two major questions: How can digital technology support environmental and social sustainability? How can digital transformation itself become more sustainable? The programme is associated with UNEP and the United Nations System Staff College and is classified as a short online learning path. Digital Accessibility and Inclusion Relevant resources include: Web Accessibility: The Cornerstone of Digital Society Inclusive Digital Communication During Crisis and Emergency Situations Smart for All: Beyond Smart Cities Inclusive Digital Economies and Gender Equality Playbook These resources focus on making digital information, services and environments accessible to people regardless of gender, age, disability, ability or context. Innovation Management and Human-Centred Design Innovation Management The UNICEF Innovation Management course is described as a nine-week online programme. It explores: Innovation concepts Idea generation Selection of ideas Strategy formulation Implementation New products and services Business models Social networks Innovation-project tools The course is designed to help learners develop an innovative mindset and understand how organizations convert ideas into practical outputs. Managing Innovation The UNIDO Managing Innovation course supports structured innovation within organizations, particularly small and medium-sized enterprises. The programme is promoted in partnership with the International Organization for Standardization and is listed as a short online course. Human-Centred Design and Service Design The World Food Programme course introduces human-centred design and service design. It explains: Why users should remain at the centre of services How service design supports technology development Why human-centred design is valuable How the approach can be applied in large organizations It is categorized as a short online course. Innovation for Impact Learning Path The UN System Staff College’s Innovation for Impact Learning Path supports capacity development across the UN system. It includes: Introduction to Innovation Learning Module UN Innovation Toolkit Learning Module Digital tools Innovation frameworks Practical innovation approaches The programme aims to improve impact and strengthen the future resilience of the UN workforce. UN Innovation Toolkit The UN Innovation Toolkit is a practical resource for supporting innovation across the United Nations system. Because it is a toolkit rather than a conventional course, it is one of the reasons the library’s result total should not be described entirely as a number of courses. Behavioural Science and Decision-Making Introduction to Behavioral Science in Public Policy This World Bank Group course introduces the role of behavioural science in policy design. It examines applications in: Education Healthcare Social norms Gender equality Tax compliance Climate action Other public-policy areas The library lists it as a short online course. Social and Behaviour Change Theory and Practice This UNICEF resource introduces social and behaviour change approaches used to generate measurable improvements in programmes and communities. It emphasizes: Evidence Participation Community engagement Human rights Behavioural outcomes Co-design Systems Change and Behavioural Science for Climate Action This course was developed by the UNFCCC Youth4Capacity programme with support from the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security and the United Nations System Staff College. It helps young leaders use: Systems thinking Behavioural science Complex-problem analysis Behavioural interventions Scalable climate solutions The library classifies it as a short online programme. Sustainable Finance, Climate Action and Environmental Innovation SDG Finance Academy E-Learning Programme The UNDP SDG Finance Academy provides self-paced modules covering different areas of sustainable finance. The programme is intended to help learners understand: Sustainable-finance concepts Financial tools Resource-mobilization strategies Financing for the Sustainable Development Goals It is listed as a short online programme connected to SDG 17. Introduction to Sustainable Finance for Climate and Energy This UNDP course is described as a free online introduction to climate and energy finance. It covers: Green bonds Blue bonds Debt-for-nature swaps Carbon markets Private-finance solutions Integrated National Financing Frameworks Impact measurement and management Investment de-risking The course is connected to SDGs 7, 13 and 17. Introduction to Impact Measurement and Management and Finance Tracking This resource introduces: Sustainability impact measurement Impact management Finance tracking Taxonomies Reporting systems Data frameworks SDG-aligned decision-making It is classified as a short online course. Nature-Based Solutions for Disaster and Climate Resilience This UNEP course explains how nature-based solutions can strengthen resilience to climate change and disasters. Participants learn about: Connections between human activity and ecological systems Disaster-risk reduction Climate resilience Ecosystem-based approaches Nature-based planning tools It is listed as a short online course linked to SDG 13. Electronic Waste Training The library includes ITU resources addressing: Global electronic-waste trends E-waste statistics Relevant legislation Data collection Environmental implications International monitoring One listed course, Addressing the E-Waste Challenge Through Data and Statistics, is categorized as a short online data-innovation course connected to climate action. Entrepreneurship and Business Innovation Innovating for Success: A Guide for Entrepreneurs This course helps aspiring young entrepreneurs understand: Entrepreneurship pathways Business risks and rewards Business start-up processes Entrepreneurial mindset Opportunity identification Innovation It is categorized as a short online programme linked to decent work and economic growth. Digital Business Innovations for Women Entrepreneurs and Managers This UNIDO course is intended to help women develop the digital knowledge and skills needed to operate and manage businesses in the Industry 4.0 environment. It is listed as a short online digital-transformation course. Green and Digital Entrepreneurship for Women The library also includes a programme addressing: Green business Circular economy Digital entrepreneurship Sustainable business operations Green supply chains Green finance Women’s economic empowerment Sector-Specific Innovation Training Agriculture and Food Systems Relevant resources include: FAO Science and Innovation Strategy Digital Agriculture Tropical Agriculture Platform Human-Centred Design and Service Design in WFP The Tropical Agriculture Platform resource contains six lessons covering: Agricultural innovation systems Capacity development Capacity-needs assessment at innovation-niche level Capacity-needs assessment at organizational level Enabling environments Monitoring, evaluation and learning Each lesson is described as requiring approximately 30 to 40 minutes. Health Health-related resources include: Digital Health: Planning National Systems Ethics and Governance of Artificial Intelligence for Health Building the Digital Economy: Pathways in Healthcare and Government These programmes examine digital-health planning, AI implementation, ethics, governance and the particular needs of developing countries. Tourism and Hospitality Tourism-related resources include: Tourism Innovation Artificial Intelligence in Hospitality: Challenges and Business Opportunities Innovation and Digital Transformation in the Tourism Industry Tourism Innovation is a four-week online course delivered by Externado University of Colombia. It covers tourism guidelines, legal frameworks, value chains and sustainable practices. Participants must obtain an 80 per cent score on the final quiz to earn a certificate. Aviation Aviation-related listings include: Innovation in Aviation Training Programme Innovation in Aviation: Fundamentals Overview of Unmanned Aviation Fundamentals These resources cover innovation concepts, regulatory challenges, organizational adaptation and unmanned aviation. Cities and Urban Mobility The library includes programmes on: Sustainable urban mobility Transit-oriented development Low-carbon transport Active mobility Inclusive transportation Digital governance in African cities Digitally accessible urban environments Blockchain, Robotics and Other Frontier Technologies Blockchain Training The library includes: The Use of Blockchain in Trade Facilitation Introduction to Blockchain for Trade Blockchain for Government These courses examine blockchain in: International trade Supply chains Digitalization Automation Government systems Public administration Blockchain for Government is specifically described as a programme helping government officials understand and use blockchain technology in public administration. Humanoid Robots at Work This ITCILO resource examines AI-powered humanoid and semi-humanoid robots. It considers: Human-machine collaboration Changes in employment Learning and education Risks Opportunities Ethics Decent work Social justice The programme is framed around the relationship between emerging technology and the future of work. Quantum for Everyone Quantum for Everyone is described as a free online course covering the basic building blocks of quantum technology. It is listed as a short online frontier-technology course. Frontier ICTs for Sustainable Development This ESCAP course examines: Artificial intelligence Blockchain Internet of Things Policy risks Good practices International cooperation Sustainable development It is particularly relevant to digital leaders and policymakers in developing countries. UN 2.0 Learning Resources The UN 2.0 learning resources focus on five cross-cutting competence areas known as the Quintet of Change: Innovation Strategic foresight Behavioural science Data Digital transformation UN 2.0 Fundamentals E-Learning Path The United Nations System Staff College developed five freely available, self-paced introductory modules covering the Quintet of Change. The learning path is listed as a long online resource. UN 2.0 Learning Path: The Quintet of Change The UNICEF learning path begins with an introduction to UN 2.0 and then explores the five competence areas. It includes: LinkedIn courses Advanced learning paths UN policies UN strategies Practical tools UNICEF learning resources It is categorized as a short online learning path. Are the Training Resources Free? The library does not provide one universal fee policy that applies to every listing. Some individual descriptions expressly state that their courses or resources are free. Examples include: Elements of AI Introduction to Sustainable Finance for Climate and Energy Quantum for Everyone Micro Training of Trainers UN 2.0 Fundamentals E-Learning Path Google Cloud Generative AI Training Resources OpenAI Explained Other listings do not state their fees directly on the library page. Prospective learners should therefore open the individual programme page and confirm: Registration fees Tuition charges Certificate fees Institutional-access requirements Nomination requirements Travel costs for in-person programmes Technology requirements Current availability Are Certificates Available? There is no universal certificate policy for the entire library. Some listings are specifically structured as professional certificate programmes, including: UN Data Analytics Professional Certificate UN Generative AI Professional Certificate Other courses may award a certificate after completion or assessment. For example, the Tourism Innovation course requires participants to achieve an 80 per cent score on the final quiz. Learners should verify the following on the individual provider’s page: Whether a certificate is offered Whether the certificate is free or paid Attendance requirements Assessment requirements Minimum passing score Assignment requirements Certificate-verification arrangements Application Deadline There is no single deadline for the entire UN Innovation Training Library. It is a collection of independently managed resources, so application dates and registration periods vary. How to Access a Training Resource There is no single application form for the entire library. Users should follow these steps: Visit the UN Innovation Training Library. Review the available resource gallery. Select a relevant Sustainable Development Goal if applicable. Filter by UN entity or provider. Choose an innovation subject. Select the preferred time requirement. Choose online, in-person or hybrid delivery. Open the selected listing. Read its complete description. Follow the link to the original provider. Confirm that the programme is still available. Review eligibility requirements. Check the deadline. Verify the fee and certificate conditions. Complete registration through the provider’s official platform. Who Should Explore the Library? The library may be useful for: United Nations personnel Government officials Policymakers Public administrators Development practitioners Humanitarian professionals Monitoring and evaluation specialists Data analysts Information management professionals Researchers Students Educators Trainers Facilitators Technology professionals Digital-transformation teams Sustainability professionals Climate-action practitioners Entrepreneurs Business managers Healthcare professionals Agriculture professionals Aviation professionals Tourism professionals Innovation managers Organizational leaders However, inclusion in this general audience does not mean that every person is eligible for every listed programme. Some resources are designed for the general public, while others target specific groups such as: UN personnel Government policymakers Women entrepreneurs Young leaders Developing-country officials Trainers and facilitators UN-led innovation teams Professionals working in particular sectors Important Points to Verify Before Enrolling Before registering for any resource, users should verify: Whether the programme is currently open. Whether it is a course, toolkit, fellowship or certificate. Whether participation is open to the public. Whether UN employment is required. Whether nomination is required. Whether there is a fee. Whether a certificate is provided. Whether the course is fully online. Whether live attendance is required. Whether the programme has regional restrictions. Whether it requires previous technical knowledge. Whether its duration remains accurate. Whether the linked external page is active. Whether a stated intake date has already passed. Frequently Asked Questions Is the UN Innovation Training Library a single programme? No. It is a searchable collection of independent courses, toolkits, learning paths, professional certificates and related resources. Does the library contain only courses? No. The gallery also contains toolkits, fellowships, accelerator programmes, certificate programmes and other professional-learning resources. Why should the number of courses not be placed in the headline? The webpage’s result counter reflects the total number of gallery entries at a particular time. It does not mean that every result is a conventional course, and the total may change because the platform is a living library. Are all resources open to the general public? No universal access rule is stated. Some are open broadly, while others are intended for UN personnel, government officials, women, young leaders or other specific audiences. Are all courses free? No universal fee policy is provided. Some resources are expressly described as free, while the costs of others must be checked on their respective provider pages. Is there one application deadline? No. Each independently managed programme may have its own deadline or registration status. Are certificates available? Certificates are available through selected programmes, but certificate requirements differ. Are the resources online? Many are online, while others are in-person or hybrid. How long do the programmes take? The library classifies resources as short, medium, long or very long, ranging from a few hours to several months. Conclusion The UN Innovation Training Library is a broad professional-development resource for people seeking to build knowledge and practical capabilities in innovation, artificial intelligence, data, digital transformation, behavioural science, sustainable finance and emerging technologies. Its strength lies in the variety of learning formats represented in the library. Users can find short introductory resources, detailed online courses, in-person training, hybrid programmes, professional certificates, toolkits, learning paths, fellowships and accelerator opportunities. The platform should not be presented as a single application opportunity or as a fixed collection of a particular number of courses. It is more accurately described as a living library of courses, toolkits and professional learning resources from across the United Nations system. There is no general deadline, fee, certificate policy or eligibility requirement applying to all listings. Each resource must be reviewed individually to determine its current status, intended audience, application procedure, cost, duration and certificate conditions. Prospective learners should use the library’s filters to identify relevant resources and then verify all final details through the official page of the individual training provider. For more global opportunities, visit HERE . The post UN Innovation Training Library 2026: Explore Courses, Toolkits and Future-Ready Professional Skills appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Australia COP31 2026 Opportunities: Submit Australian Pavilion Event Proposals or Apply for Climate Conference Travel Support to Attend the UN Climate Summit in Türkiye
summary: As Australia prepares to play a historic leadership role at the 31st United Nations Climate Change Conference (COP31), individuals, organizations, community groups, researchers, youth leaders, First Nations representatives, businesses, and civil society organizations now have exciting opportunities to participate in one of the world s most influential climate events. The Australian Government, through the Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW), has opened applications for two major COP31 engagement opportunities: COP31 Australian Pavilion Event Proposals COP31 Climate Conference Travel Support Program These initiatives are designed to ensure Australia s participation at COP31 reflects the country s innovation, diversity, clean energy transition, and commitment to global climate action. COP31 will take place in Antalya, Türkiye , from 9 to 20 November 2026 , where Australia will serve as the President of Negotiations while partnering with Türkiye and Pacific nations to deliver one of the world s largest international climate conferences. Whether you want to organize an event at Australia s official COP31 Pavilion or seek financial support to attend the conference, these opportunities provide a valuable platform to contribute to global climate discussions and showcase Australian leadership. About COP31 The Conference of the Parties (COP) is the world s premier annual climate summit under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Each year, COP brings together: National governments International organizations Researchers Businesses Investors Civil society Indigenous communities Youth representatives Climate advocates Environmental organizations The conference serves as the primary international forum for negotiating and implementing global climate policies, including commitments under the Paris Agreement. COP31 represents an especially significant milestone for Australia because the country will play a central role in facilitating negotiations, encouraging stronger climate ambition, and supporting implementation of international climate commitments. The event will also provide Australia with an opportunity to showcase national achievements in clean energy, innovation, sustainability, and regional partnerships. Australia s Leadership Role at COP31 Australia s role extends far beyond participating as a delegation. As President of Negotiations , Australia will: Guide international climate negotiations Help facilitate consensus among participating countries Promote ambitious climate action Support implementation of the Paris Agreement Showcase Australia s climate leadership Strengthen partnerships with Türkiye and Pacific nations Highlight Australia s clean energy transition The conference also presents an opportunity to demonstrate Australia s progress in renewable energy, electrification, and sustainable development. Opportunity 1: COP31 Australian Pavilion Event Proposals One of the key opportunities currently open is the submission of proposals for events to be featured within the Australian Pavilion during COP31. What is the Australian Pavilion? Every year at COP, participating countries establish national pavilions that serve as spaces for: Panel discussions Workshops Presentations Policy dialogues Networking events Knowledge sharing Showcasing innovative projects Demonstrating climate leadership The Australian Pavilion acts as Australia s public platform for presenting its climate initiatives to an international audience. For COP31, the Pavilion will place particular emphasis on: Clean energy transition Electrification Benefits of clean energy for households Australia s partnership with Pacific nations Climate innovation Practical climate solutions Click HERE to learn more. What Can Applicants Propose? Organizations are invited to submit proposals for activities such as: Public events Panel discussions Workshops Presentations Interactive sessions Knowledge-sharing activities Climate-focused content Collaborative discussions Each proposal should demonstrate how it contributes to Australia s climate story and supports the overall objectives of COP31. Click HERE to register. Submission Guidelines Applicants should note the following: Each proposed event requires a separate application form. Organizations are encouraged to nominate one primary contact person to coordinate submissions. Full submission details are available through the COP Engagement Hub. Deadline for Pavilion Event Proposals Application Deadline: Friday, 31 July 2026 Organizations are encouraged to prepare their proposals well before the closing date. Opportunity 2: COP31 Climate Conference Travel Support Program The Australian Government has also launched the COP31 Climate Conference Travel Support Program , providing financial assistance to enable a diverse range of Australians to attend the conference in person. The initiative aims to increase participation from individuals and communities that are traditionally underrepresented in international climate negotiations. Purpose of the Travel Support Program The programme seeks to ensure Australia s delegation reflects the country s diversity by supporting representatives from different backgrounds, sectors, and regions. Rather than limiting participation to established organizations, the programme encourages broader community representation. Who Is Encouraged to Apply? Applications are welcomed from individuals and organizations across Australia, including: Young people aged 18–35 First Nations Australians Regional communities Community organizations Climate advocates Environmental organizations Researchers Businesses Civil society organizations Industry representatives Youth organizations Community leaders The programme particularly encourages applications from groups that have historically been underrepresented within the COP process. What Does the Programme Support? Successful applicants will receive travel support that enables participation in COP31. Applicants should carefully review the official Grant Opportunity Guidelines for detailed eligibility requirements and funding conditions. Click HERE to learn more. Assessment Process Applications will be evaluated according to the eligibility and assessment criteria outlined in the official grant documentation. Prospective applicants should ensure they: Meet all eligibility requirements Address every assessment criterion Submit complete supporting documentation Apply before the closing date Click HERE to register. Deadline for Travel Support Applications Application Deadline: Monday, 27 July 2026 Late applications will not be considered. Why These Opportunities Matter Participation in COP31 provides significant professional and organizational benefits. Successful participants may have opportunities to: Participate in one of the world s largest climate conferences Showcase Australian innovation internationally Build partnerships with global organizations Engage directly with policymakers Network with climate experts Contribute to implementation of the Paris Agreement Share successful Australian initiatives Strengthen Australia s international climate profile Promote clean energy solutions Engage with global sustainability leaders Australia s Focus Areas at COP31 Australia intends to highlight several priority areas during the conference, including: Clean energy transition Electrification Renewable energy Household energy transformation Climate innovation Regional cooperation Pacific partnerships Practical climate solutions Sustainable development International collaboration How to Prepare a Strong Application Applicants seeking to maximize their chances of success should consider the following recommendations: For Australian Pavilion Proposals Align your event with Australia s climate priorities. Demonstrate clear relevance to COP31 themes. Highlight collaboration and innovation. Explain the expected outcomes and audience engagement. Ensure the proposal complements Australia s Pavilion programme. For Travel Support Applications Clearly demonstrate how attending COP31 will contribute to your work or community. Explain your organization s climate-related activities or experience. Address every assessment criterion thoroughly. Provide complete supporting documentation. Submit your application before the deadline. Read more HERE Important Dates Activity Deadline COP31 Climate Conference Travel Support Program 27 July 2026 Australian Pavilion Event Proposal Submission 31 July 2026 COP31 Conference 9–20 November 2026 How to Apply Interested applicants should: Determine which opportunity best matches their interests. Carefully review the eligibility requirements. Prepare all required information and supporting documents. Submit their application through the relevant official application process before the deadline. Consult the official guidance documents if additional clarification is needed. Applicants seeking travel support should review the official Grant Opportunity Guidelines before preparing their submission. Why You Should Apply Australia s participation in COP31 presents a unique opportunity for organizations and individuals to help shape the country s contribution to one of the world s most important climate conferences. Whether by organizing events within the Australian Pavilion or participating through the Travel Support Program, successful applicants will contribute to international discussions on climate policy, renewable energy, sustainability, and implementation of the Paris Agreement. These opportunities also provide valuable exposure, networking, and collaboration with governments, researchers, industry leaders, civil society organizations, and climate experts from around the world. Conclusion The Australian Government s COP31 engagement opportunities offer an exceptional chance for organizations, young people, First Nations communities, researchers, businesses, and climate leaders to become active participants in global climate action. With Australia serving as President of Negotiations at COP31, these initiatives aim to showcase the country s leadership, innovation, and commitment to achieving meaningful climate outcomes. Prospective applicants are encouraged to review the official guidance carefully, prepare strong submissions, and apply before the respective deadlines. Whether contributing through an Australian Pavilion event or attending the conference with travel support, participants will have the opportunity to help shape Australia s presence at one of the most significant international climate events of the decade. Discover more global Grant and Conference opportunities on OFY. The post Australia COP31 2026 Opportunities: Submit Australian Pavilion Event Proposals or Apply for Climate Conference Travel Support to Attend the UN Climate Summit in Türkiye appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 in Ghana: Capacity Building, Mentorship and Grant Funding for Youth-Led Organizations
summary: Youth-led organizations and social enterprises in Ghana now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 , an initiative under the EU-Ghana Youth Empowerment Programme . Implemented by SOS Children s Villages Ghana in partnership with the European Union and other collaborating organizations, the programme is designed to empower youth-led and youth-focused civil society organizations (CSOs) and social enterprises with the knowledge, skills, mentorship, and technical support they need to expand their impact and contribute to sustainable development. Beyond training and organizational development, the programme also provides successful participants with the opportunity to compete for grant funding , enabling them to scale their projects and create lasting change within their communities. If your organization is passionate about empowering young people, promoting innovation, protecting the environment, or strengthening communities, this programme offers an excellent platform to build capacity and access future funding opportunities. About the SPARK Change-Makers Project The SPARK Change-Makers Project is a comprehensive organizational development programme that seeks to strengthen youth-led organizations before providing access to financial support. Rather than offering immediate grants, the programme adopts a phased approach , ensuring participating organizations first receive intensive capacity-building support to improve their effectiveness, governance, sustainability, and long-term impact. Selected organizations will participate in a structured programme that equips them with practical skills, strategic guidance, and expert support before becoming eligible to compete for grant funding. The initiative focuses on empowering young people between the ages of 18 and 35 by strengthening the organizations that serve and represent them. Programme Benefits Organizations selected for the SPARK Change-Makers Project will receive a wide range of support designed to strengthen their operations and increase their impact. Benefits include: Comprehensive organizational capacity development. Leadership and organizational skills training. One-on-one coaching from experienced professionals. Expert mentorship throughout the programme. Technical assistance and advisory support. Networking opportunities with partners and stakeholders. Access to future grant funding opportunities. Support for developing sustainable income-generating activities. Improved organizational sustainability and long-term growth. Increased ability to deliver impactful community projects. Opportunity to Access Grant Funding One of the major highlights of the SPARK Change-Makers Project is the opportunity to access grant funding. Organizations that successfully complete the programme s capacity-strengthening phase become eligible to compete for grants that can help expand and scale their initiatives. This ensures that participating organizations are better prepared to manage funding effectively while maximizing community impact. Who Can Apply? The programme is open to organizations that are youth-led or youth-focused and operate within the programme s six implementing regions of Ghana. Eligible applicants include: Youth-led civil society organizations (CSOs). Youth-focused civil society organizations. Youth-led social enterprises. Youth-focused social enterprises. Priority Focus Areas The SPARK Change-Makers Project supports organizations working in sectors that contribute to youth empowerment and sustainable development. Priority areas include: 1. Gender Empowerment Projects that promote gender equality, women s empowerment, inclusion, and equal opportunities for young people. 2. Agriculture and Environmental Protection Initiatives focused on sustainable agriculture, climate resilience, environmental conservation, food security, and green innovation. 3. Peace and Security Projects that promote peaceful communities, conflict prevention, civic participation, and social cohesion. 4. Culture and Creative Industries Organizations supporting arts, culture, creative entrepreneurship, and cultural preservation among young people. 5. Digital Technology and Innovation Projects leveraging digital tools, technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship to improve lives and create opportunities for youth. Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors. Strong applications should demonstrate: Youth leadership within the organization. Clear organizational goals and objectives. Active community engagement. Commitment to inclusion and diversity. Environmental sustainability. Potential for long-term impact. Organizational readiness for growth and scaling. Organizations that clearly demonstrate these qualities will be well positioned during the selection process. Why This Programme Matters Many youth-led organizations possess innovative ideas and strong community connections but often face challenges such as limited organizational capacity, inadequate technical support, and restricted access to funding. The SPARK Change-Makers Project addresses these challenges by investing in organizational development before providing access to financial resources. Participants gain practical knowledge, professional guidance, and stronger institutional capacity, enabling them to build sustainable organizations that continue delivering impact long after the programme ends. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 31 July 2026 Interested organizations should complete and submit their applications before the deadline to be considered for the programme. Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Apply If your organization is committed to creating positive change for young people in Ghana, the SPARK Change-Makers Project offers far more than funding. It provides the skills, mentorship, networks, and institutional support needed to build a stronger, more sustainable organization capable of delivering lasting impact. Whether you work in gender equality, environmental sustainability, peacebuilding, the creative economy, or digital innovation, this programme can help you strengthen your organization while positioning it for future grant opportunities. Click here for more global grant opportunities The post SPARK Change-Makers Project 2026 in Ghana: Capacity Building, Mentorship and Grant Funding for Youth-Led Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UN Women Opens Applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 on Gender-Responsive and Inclusive Skills Development
summary: The United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women) , in partnership with the German Agency for International Cooperation (GIZ) through the Regional Cooperation on Labour Market Policy and Technical and Vocational Education and Training for a Twin Transition in ASEAN (RECOTwin) project, has officially opened applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles (WEPs) Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 . Applications are open from 15 July to 15 August 2026 . This six-month innovation programme is designed for businesses that are ready to strengthen gender equality while building a future-ready workforce capable of thriving in the country s rapidly expanding green and digital economy. The initiative comes at a critical time as the Philippines undergoes its green and digital Twin Transition, creating new employment opportunities while also exposing significant skills shortages across multiple sectors. At the same time, many talented individuals—including women, young people, LGBTQIA+ persons, people with disabilities and other underrepresented groups—continue to experience barriers that prevent them from accessing quality skills development and employment opportunities. The WEPs Corporate Action Lab seeks to help businesses address these challenges by developing innovative, practical and measurable solutions that promote inclusive skills development and equitable career pathways. About the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab The Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab (WEPsCAL) is a cohort-based programme that supports companies over a period of approximately six months. Rather than offering theoretical discussions, the programme functions as an innovation laboratory where participating companies design, pilot, evaluate and improve real workplace solutions tailored to their own organizational needs. Participants receive expert guidance throughout the programme while collaborating with other businesses that are equally committed to promoting gender equality and inclusive employment. The programme is implemented under the UN Women Gender Action Lab (GAL): Innovation and Impact for Gender Equality in the Asia-Pacific, in partnership with Australia s Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. Why Inclusive Skills Development Matters The Philippines is experiencing rapid transformation driven by green technologies and digital innovation. Although these developments are creating thousands of new career opportunities, employers continue to report significant challenges in finding workers with the required skills. At the same time, structural inequalities continue to prevent many talented individuals from accessing technical education, vocational training and quality employment. UN Women believes that businesses can address these challenges while simultaneously improving productivity, innovation and long-term competitiveness by embedding Gender Equality, Disability and Social Inclusion (GEDSI) principles into workplace learning and career development. Gender-responsive and inclusive training programmes can help businesses: Build stronger talent pipelines. Improve employee retention. Increase productivity. Encourage workplace innovation. Develop a workforce prepared for future labour market demands. Support sustainable economic growth. Focus of the 2026 Corporate Action Lab The selected companies will work together to answer the following challenge: How can businesses design and implement skills development initiatives that create equitable career pathways for women and diverse talent during the green and digital transitions? Throughout the programme, participants will receive technical support to develop practical solutions that can be tested within their organizations. Objectives of the Programme The Corporate Action Lab will support participating companies to: Analyse the business case for gender-responsive training by demonstrating its financial and operational value. Identify and eliminate unconscious bias within corporate training materials, learning environments and delivery approaches. Remove systemic barriers preventing women and diverse learners from entering technical and emerging occupations. Design, pilot and evaluate innovative workplace solutions tailored to their specific organizational contexts. Develop measurable Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to monitor progress and sustain inclusive training standards. Share successful practices with stakeholders across the Philippines and ASEAN to encourage wider policy adoption and scaling. Benefits of Participating Selected companies will receive numerous benefits throughout the programme, including: Access to the Women’s Empowerment Principles Gender Gap Analysis Tool. Use of exclusive WEPs resources. Development of customized organizational workplans. Individual technical assistance and coaching sessions. Innovation and problem-solving support. Peer learning with other participating businesses. Networking opportunities with experts and solution providers. Access to innovators working on gender equality and inclusive employment. National recognition. Regional recognition. Global recognition upon successful completion of the programme. Number of Companies to be Selected UN Women plans to select 8–12 companies for the 2026 Philippines Corporate Action Lab. Information Sessions Interested organizations are encouraged to attend one of the free online information sessions before submitting their applications. The sessions will explain: Programme requirements Eligibility criteria Benefits of participation Application process Selection process Information session dates include: 22 July 2026 29 July 2026 10 August 2026 Each session will be held online via Microsoft Teams. Application Process Interested companies should complete the official online application form before the deadline. After applications are reviewed, UN Women will contact successful applicants via email. Successful participants are expected to be notified in early September 2026 , while the official programme launch is tentatively scheduled for the second week of September 2026 . Application Deadline 15 August 2026 How to Apply Eligible companies should complete the official online application form through the UN Women application portal before the deadline. For additional information, applicants are encouraged to review the Challenge Brief, Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs), and attend one of the scheduled information sessions before submitting their applications. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here . Discover more Global Training Opportunities here . The post UN Women Opens Applications for the Women’s Empowerment Principles Corporate Action Lab Philippines 2026 on Gender-Responsive and Inclusive Skills Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SEACAF 2026 Research Fellowship: Receive Up to USD 15,000 to Advance Academic Freedom Research in Southeast Asia
summary: The Southeast Asia Coalition for Academic Freedom (SEACAF) has officially opened applications for its 2026 Research Fellowships on Empowering Academic Freedom in Southeast Asia , offering funding of up to USD 15,000 for research projects that address some of the most pressing challenges facing higher education and academic freedom across the region. This prestigious fellowship is designed to support early-career researchers, academics, and research advocates committed to producing evidence-based research that strengthens academic freedom, promotes democratic values, and informs policy reforms throughout Southeast Asia. Each year, the programme selects six fellows who will participate in a structured fellowship lasting between 10 and 12 months , combining financial support, expert mentorship, peer learning, and opportunities to contribute meaningful policy recommendations that can influence higher education systems across the region. About the SEACAF Research Fellowship Academic freedom remains one of the foundations of quality higher education. It enables scholars, educators, and students to freely teach, research, publish, and engage in critical inquiry without fear of censorship or retaliation. Across Southeast Asia, however, academic freedom faces increasing pressure from democratic backsliding, government restrictions, institutional control, and broader political challenges. Academics and students who engage in research or public discussions on social justice, governance, human rights, and public policy increasingly encounter obstacles such as: State surveillance Administrative sanctions Restrictions on teaching and research Criminalization of dissent Limitations on publishing academic work Political interference in universities Constraints arising from national security policies Increased risks in conflict-affected areas The SEACAF Research Fellowship seeks to generate high-quality research that helps governments, universities, civil society organizations, and policymakers better understand these challenges while developing practical solutions that strengthen academic freedom across Southeast Asia. Fellowship Themes Applicants are invited to submit research proposals under one of three priority themes. Theme 1: Neoliberal Higher Education and Its Impacts on Academic Freedom Across Southeast Asia, universities increasingly operate under market-driven models that emphasize: Revenue generation Competition Commercialization International rankings Performance indicators While these reforms aim to improve institutional competitiveness, they may also weaken: Academic independence Institutional autonomy Research integrity Public service missions of universities Freedom of teaching and inquiry Research under this theme should use practical case studies to examine how neoliberal higher education policies affect academic freedom in real-world settings. The findings are expected to contribute to policy recommendations that promote universities as spaces for independent thinking, critical knowledge production, and public engagement. Funding: Up to USD 12,000 Duration: 10 months Application Type: Individual or team Theme 2: Developing Regional Indicators for Academic Freedom Measuring academic freedom requires reliable and context-specific indicators. This fellowship theme supports research that works closely with stakeholders across Southeast Asia to develop: Regional academic freedom indicators Monitoring frameworks Documentation systems Context-sensitive assessment tools Regional policy recommendations The long-term objective is to establish a comprehensive academic freedom monitoring system throughout Southeast Asia.
