Funding: Up to USD 12,000 Duration: 10 months Application Type: Individual or team Theme 3: Participatory Action Research for Training Modules on Academic Freedom This theme focuses on developing practical educational resources that strengthen understanding of academic freedom among universities and student communities. Research teams will collaborate directly with academic institutions, educators, and students to: Develop training modules Produce educational materials Test learning resources through participatory workshops Improve awareness of academic freedom Unlike the other themes, this fellowship requires collaborative research. Funding: Up to USD 15,000 Duration: 12 months Application Type: Team application only (minimum of three members) Fellowship Benefits Successful fellows will receive much more than financial support. Benefits include: Funding of up to USD 15,000 depending on the fellowship theme. Participation in a 10–12 month structured fellowship . Expert mentorship from experienced researchers and practitioners. Peer learning with researchers from across Southeast Asia. Opportunities to produce impactful research. Development of policy recommendations for governments and higher education institutions. Contribution to strengthening academic freedom across the region. Increased professional visibility and networking opportunities. What Can the Fellowship Funding Cover? The fellowship provides flexible funding to support research activities. Eligible expenses include: Researcher s Allowance Monthly stipend or compensation for researchers throughout the fellowship period. Research Expenses Funding may be used for costs directly associated with conducting research. Fieldwork and Travel Support includes: Transportation Accommodation Field visits Other fieldwork-related costs Research Assistants Funding may cover payments for research assistants supporting project implementation. Data Collection Eligible costs include: Surveys Interviews Transcription services Data acquisition Other research-related data collection activities Why Academic Freedom Matters Academic freedom allows universities to fulfill their public mission by encouraging independent thinking, innovation, and evidence-based research. Without academic freedom: Research quality declines. Public debate becomes limited. Universities lose institutional independence. Innovation and creativity suffer. Democratic participation weakens. The SEACAF fellowship seeks to strengthen higher education systems where scholars can freely pursue knowledge while contributing to more democratic and inclusive societies. Selection Information For the 2026 fellowship cohort: Only six fellows will be selected. Proposals aligned with the three priority themes will receive the highest consideration. Proposals outside these themes may still be submitted but will receive lower priority during the review process. Research Duration Depending on the selected theme, fellows will conduct research over: 10 months (Themes 1 and 2) 12 months (Theme 3) Throughout the fellowship, participants will receive structured support while conducting original research and producing policy-oriented outputs. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 20 July 2026 Applicants are encouraged to prepare their proposals early and submit all required documents before the deadline. Click here for more and to apply Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about higher education, human rights, democratic governance, or public policy in Southeast Asia, the SEACAF Research Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to conduct meaningful research while influencing regional policy discussions. Beyond funding, fellows become part of a growing regional network committed to protecting academic freedom, strengthening higher education institutions, and ensuring that universities remain places where knowledge, critical inquiry, and truth can flourish. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post SEACAF 2026 Research Fellowship: Receive Up to USD 15,000 to Advance Academic Freedom Research in Southeast Asia appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum by the German Marshall Fund of the United States: A Global Fellowship for Emerging Strategic Leaders in Indo-Pacific Security
summary: The 2027 Young Strategists Forum (YSF) by the German Marshall Fund of the United States (GMF) is now accepting applications from emerging leaders who are interested in strengthening their strategic thinking skills and contributing to discussions on international security, diplomacy, and global affairs. The Young Strategists Forum is a prestigious leadership development program designed to prepare the next generation of strategic thinkers for a world facing increasingly complex geopolitical challenges, limited resources, and evolving security dynamics. The program provides participants with a unique platform to engage with experts, policymakers, diplomats, academics, and fellow emerging leaders from across the world. Funded by and conducted in partnership with the Sasakawa Peace Foundation , the program focuses on strategic issues surrounding the United States-Japan alliance and security dynamics in the Indo-Pacific region . Through intensive learning activities, simulations, expert discussions, and networking opportunities, participants gain practical experience in analyzing international challenges and developing innovative policy solutions. About the Young Strategists Forum Since its establishment in March 2012, the Young Strategists Forum has developed into a highly respected platform for cultivating future foreign policy and security leaders. The program has created a strong international network of professionals who continue to influence policy discussions and strategic decision-making in their respective fields. The fellowship is built around the idea that today’s global challenges require leaders who can think strategically, collaborate across borders, and understand the connections between security, economics, technology, and diplomacy. The 2027 edition will continue this mission by bringing together talented professionals from different sectors and regions to examine emerging geopolitical issues and explore pathways toward international cooperation. Program Focus: Strategy, Competition, and Cooperation in the Indo-Pacific The Young Strategists Forum places significant emphasis on the changing security environment in the Indo-Pacific region. Participants will explore major global issues affecting international relations, including: The future of the United States-Japan alliance Regional security challenges in the Indo-Pacific International cooperation and strategic competition The role of emerging leaders in shaping foreign policy Long-term approaches to global stability and security The program encourages participants to understand complex global issues from multiple perspectives and develop strategic approaches to addressing international challenges. Fellowship Activities and Program Format The Young Strategists Forum provides a comprehensive learning experience combining academic discussions, practical exercises, and professional networking. Selected participants will take part in activities including: 1. Expert Lectures and Strategic Discussions Participants engage with leading experts, academics, diplomats, and policymakers who provide insights into international affairs, security policy, and strategic decision-making. 2. 36-Hour Simulation Exercise One of the central components of the program is an intensive simulation exercise designed to challenge participants’ analytical skills, teamwork abilities, and strategic decision-making under pressure. Through this exercise, participants experience realistic policy scenarios and develop solutions to complex international problems. 3. Meetings with Global Leaders Participants have opportunities to interact with: Policymakers Diplomats Senior journalists Leading academics Strategic experts These discussions allow fellows to gain firsthand perspectives on global affairs and leadership challenges. 4. Study Tour Experience The program also includes a study tour featuring visits to important institutions, including a military facility, allowing participants to gain practical understanding of security and defense issues. Who Can Apply for the Young Strategists Forum? The program is open to emerging leaders between the ages of 28 and 42 who demonstrate professional experience and interest in strategic issues. Eligible applicants may include: Academics Journalists Policymakers Politicians Business professionals Military officers Applicants should demonstrate leadership potential, professional achievement, and an interest in international affairs, security, and strategic policy discussions. Eligible Countries and Regions The fellowship welcomes applicants from: The United States European countries Japan Other like-minded Asian countries The program aims to build a diverse international network of professionals who can collaborate across regions and contribute to global strategic discussions. Benefits of Participating in the Young Strategists Forum Successful participants will gain several valuable opportunities, including: 1. Leadership Development Participants strengthen their ability to analyze complex challenges, develop strategic solutions, and lead discussions on international issues. 2. Global Professional Network The fellowship connects participants with a global community of emerging leaders, experts, policymakers, and alumni working in strategic fields. Since its creation, 162 individuals have participated in the Young Strategists Forum , with many alumni advancing into influential positions within their professions. 3. Exposure to International Policy Discussions Participants gain direct exposure to discussions involving global security, diplomacy, foreign policy, and international cooperation. 4. Practical Strategic Skills Through simulations and expert-led sessions, fellows develop practical skills in: Strategic analysis Decision-making Policy development International collaboration Crisis management 5. Access to a Prestigious Alumni Network Graduates become part of a growing network of professionals who continue engaging in global policy discussions and strategic initiatives. Application Deadline The application process for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum has two important deadlines: New registrations must be completed by Thursday, August 13, 2026, at 11:59 AM ET / 17:59 CEST. Completed applications must be submitted by Friday, August 14, 2026, at 11:59 AM ET / 17:59 CEST. Applications submitted after these deadlines will not be accepted. How to Apply Interested candidates can submit their applications through the official application portal: APPLY HERE Applicants experiencing login or application issues can contact the program team through the provided contact information: JPallanch@gmfus.org Final Thoughts The Young Strategists Forum represents a valuable opportunity for emerging leaders who want to expand their understanding of global security challenges and develop the strategic skills needed in today’s rapidly changing world. By combining expert discussions, simulations, international networking, and leadership development, the fellowship equips participants with the knowledge and connections needed to contribute meaningfully to future policy conversations. Professionals interested in international affairs, diplomacy, security studies, and strategic leadership are encouraged to prepare their applications before the deadline. For more information about this opportunity, click here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities here . The post Apply for the 2027 Young Strategists Forum by the German Marshall Fund of the United States: A Global Fellowship for Emerging Strategic Leaders in Indo-Pacific Security appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: British Council Travel Grant Fund 2026: Funding for UK Filmmakers and XR Creatives to Attend International Festivals, Labs and Co-Production Forums
summary: The British Council Travel Grant Fund is an exciting funding opportunity designed to help filmmakers and XR (Extended Reality) creatives from the United Kingdom expand their careers by participating in leading international festivals, showcase events, creative development labs, and co-production forums. Supported through BFI National Lottery funding , in partnership with BFI NETWORK and the BFI International Fund , this initiative aims to strengthen the global presence of UK creative talent by making international networking and showcasing opportunities more accessible. The fund recognizes that attending major international industry events can significantly influence a filmmaker s career. Whether presenting a short film, screening a feature film, showcasing an immersive XR project, or seeking production partners for a new feature, the Travel Grant Fund provides financial support that enables creatives to represent their work on the international stage. Unlike many funding opportunities that operate under fixed application windows, the British Council Travel Grant Fund operates as a rolling fund , allowing eligible applicants to submit applications throughout the year before their intended travel. About the British Council Travel Grant Fund International festivals, film markets, labs, and co-production forums provide filmmakers with opportunities to: Showcase their creative work to international audiences. Meet producers, distributors, financiers, and collaborators. Build long-term professional networks. Discover international market opportunities. Develop future film projects. Increase the visibility of UK film and XR talent worldwide. Learn from global industry experts. Secure partnerships and funding for future productions. The Travel Grant Fund was established to remove some of the financial barriers that prevent filmmakers and XR creatives from participating in these important international events. The programme particularly focuses on increasing the international exposure of new and emerging UK filmmakers and XR creators , helping them build sustainable international careers. What Does the Travel Grant Fund Support? The fund supports travel to selected international events through five funding strands , grouped under two major application categories. 1. Festivals and Showcases Applicants wishing to attend international festivals or showcase events can apply through one of the following grants. Short Film Festival Grants These grants are available for: New and emerging writers New and emerging directors Producers of any experience level The funding supports attendance at eligible international festivals where a short film is being screened. This grant enables filmmakers to: Attend film screenings. Meet festival programmers. Connect with distributors. Build international industry relationships. Promote their short films to wider audiences. Feature Film Festival Grants This funding is specifically designed for producers attending eligible international festivals where a feature film is being screened. The grant helps producers: Represent their feature film internationally. Meet sales agents. Connect with distributors. Build financing opportunities. Expand international partnerships. Interactive, Immersive and XR Grants This funding supports XR creatives attending eligible international festivals and showcase events with interactive, immersive, or XR projects. Eligible projects may include: Virtual Reality (VR) Augmented Reality (AR) Mixed Reality (MR) Interactive storytelling Immersive digital experiences Recipients can showcase innovative work while building valuable international collaborations. 2. Labs and Co-Production Forums The second application route focuses on supporting feature film development through international labs and co-production forums. Lab Grants Lab Grants are intended for: Producers Writers Directors Applicants must be working on new feature film projects and attending eligible international creative development labs. These labs provide opportunities to: Develop projects with experienced mentors. Refine scripts. Improve production strategies. Receive professional feedback. Build relationships with international industry professionals. Co-Production Forum Grants These grants are available for producers seeking international finance and production partners for feature film projects. The funding supports attendance at eligible international markets and co-production forums where producers can: Present projects to investors. Meet international production partners. Secure financing opportunities. Expand international co-production networks. Eligible International Events Applicants can only receive funding for approved international events. The British Council maintains Key Lists of eligible: Short film festivals Feature film festivals XR festivals and showcase events Creative development labs Co-production forums These lists are carefully curated to include: Major international film events Leading XR festivals Industry-recognized development programmes Specialist events supporting diverse creative voices The Key Lists are reviewed every six months, and all travel awards remain subject to available funding. Benefits of the British Council Travel Grant Fund Successful applicants benefit in many ways beyond financial assistance. Key benefits include: Financial support for international travel. Increased international exposure. Opportunities to showcase films and XR projects. Access to leading international festivals. Professional networking opportunities. Connections with producers, distributors, financiers, and collaborators. Career development opportunities. Participation in internationally recognized creative labs. Access to co-production markets. Increased visibility for UK creative talent. Support for emerging filmmakers entering international markets. Accessibility and Inclusive Support The British Council is committed to making its funding programmes accessible to everyone. Applicants requiring additional assistance can request support throughout the application process. Available support includes: Funding guidelines in alternative formats such as large print or audio. Assistance understanding application guidelines. Help completing application forms. Additional travel funding for: British Sign Language (BSL) interpreters. Access workers. Applicants with accessibility needs are encouraged to contact the British Council before submitting their application. Support requests can be made by emailing: Films@britishcouncil.org Sustainability Commitment The British Council recognizes that international travel contributes to environmental challenges. However, it also acknowledges that face-to-face participation remains invaluable for filmmakers and XR creatives, particularly those at the beginning of their careers. To encourage more sustainable travel practices, the programme has partnered with Julie s Bicycle to produce a Greener Travel Grants Guide . Recipients travelling to European events are encouraged to use rail travel wherever possible. Additional funding is also available to support train travel to eligible European events. Funding Partners The Travel Grant Fund is made possible through collaboration between: BFI National Lottery BFI NETWORK BFI International Fund British Council Together, these organizations help strengthen the international reach of UK film and immersive creative industries. Application Process The Travel Grant Fund has been designed with a straightforward application process. Applicants simply choose the funding strand that matches their project and event before submitting their application ahead of travel. The two main application routes are: Festivals and Showcases Short Film Festival Grants Feature Film Festival Grants Interactive/Immersive/XR Grants Labs and Co-Production Forums Lab Grants Co-Production Forum Grants Applications are assessed according to eligibility requirements and available funding. Important Deadline There is no fixed application deadline. The British Council Travel Grant Fund operates as a rolling funding programme , meaning applications are accepted throughout the year. However, applicants must submit their application before travelling . Funding cannot be awarded for festivals, labs, showcases, or events that have already taken place. Why You Should Apply Participating in internationally recognized festivals, development labs, and co-production forums can transform a filmmaker s career. Beyond financial assistance, the British Council Travel Grant Fund provides access to opportunities that foster international recognition, industry partnerships, creative growth, and future funding prospects. Whether you are screening a short film, launching a feature, showcasing an immersive XR project, or developing your next production with global collaborators, this programme offers valuable support to help UK creative professionals reach wider audiences and build lasting international careers. Interested applicants should review the eligible event lists, select the appropriate funding strand, and submit their application before making travel arrangements. title: Palladium Junior Professional Opportunity in Ghana 2026: Build Your Career in Sustainable Land Use, Climate, and International Development
summary: Palladium is recruiting a Junior Professional to support the West Africa team working on the Investments in Forests and Sustainable Land Use Programme – Phase 2 (IFSLU2) . The successful candidate will be based primarily in: Location: Accra, Ghana Work arrangement: Full-time position (40 hours per week) Expected start date: October 1, 2026 Programme duration: One-year professional development opportunity The role provides hands-on experience in: Sustainable land-use projects Climate and environmental programmes Investment development Business incubation Grant management Research and analysis Project coordination Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) The Junior Professional will work closely with teams responsible for grants, communications, monitoring, evaluation, learning, and external relations. About the Investments in Forests and Sustainable Land Use Programme – Phase 2 (IFSLU2) The IFSLU2 programme is one of Palladium’s flagship initiatives under its Natural Capital and Climate, Environment and Natural Resources practice areas. The programme aims to support businesses, investors, governments, and communities in developing sustainable economic opportunities connected to forests and land use. The initiative focuses on creating investment models where economic growth can happen while also: Protecting existing forests Restoring degraded land Improving agricultural production practices Supporting sustainable businesses Increasing private-sector investment in environmentally responsible activities The programme operates across tropical forest regions and is funded by the United Kingdom government through the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO). IFSLU2 began in February 2025 and is planned to operate for five years, with possible extension depending on performance and funding availability. Key Responsibilities of the Junior Professional The selected candidate will support the West Africa team in several important areas. 1. Project Development Support Responsibilities include: Conducting market research and value-chain analysis to support sustainable business projects. Supporting business diagnostics and feasibility assessments. Preparing business plans and economic analyses. Evaluating investment opportunities and project sustainability. Supporting innovative solutions that encourage private-sector investment in forestry and sustainable land use. Assisting with the preparation of project concept notes and idea documents. Reviewing project proposals submitted by partners and improving them according to programme requirements. Supporting the inclusion of gender considerations and safeguarding requirements in project planning. Working with monitoring and evaluation teams to identify expected project outcomes. Participating in stakeholder meetings and documenting important discussions. 2. Project Management Support The Junior Professional will assist with effective project implementation through activities such as: Supporting project monitoring and reporting. Reviewing technical and financial reports. Working with grant recipients to resolve reporting challenges. Supporting payment processes, including verifying payment information and preparing documentation. Helping identify lessons learned and impact stories from projects. Maintaining communication with selected project partners. Supporting regular engagement and problem-solving activities. 3. Reporting and Programme Coordination The role will also involve: Supporting the preparation of client reports and internal programme updates. Organising meetings, events, and coordination activities. Preparing documentation for project approval committees. Maintaining programme records and information systems. Uploading research documents and reports into platforms such as SharePoint and Monday.com. Supporting quarterly, annual, and special reporting requirements. Contributing to technical reports and programme deliverables. Required Qualifications and Skills Applicants should demonstrate strong academic preparation and professional potential. Educational Requirements Candidates should have a: Bachelor’s degree or Master’s degree in: Economics Finance Agribusiness Environmental Economics International Development Environmental Studies Public Policy International Relations Related fields Technical Skills Required Successful applicants should have: Strong analytical and research abilities. Experience conducting financial analysis or market assessments. Ability to prepare business plans, feasibility studies, or investment cases. Strong Microsoft Office skills, especially: Microsoft Excel Microsoft PowerPoint Excellent written and verbal English communication skills. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Personal Qualities Palladium is seeking candidates who are: Adaptable and open to new approaches. Innovative problem-solvers. Strong communicators. Able to build professional relationships. Responsible and accountable. Passionate about climate, sustainability, and international development. Additional Preferred Qualifications Although not mandatory, candidates with the following experience may have an advantage: Experience working with international development organisations. Experience supporting FCDO-funded programmes. Background in environmental studies or sustainable agriculture. Knowledge of forest-risk commodities such as: Rubber Cashew Oil palm French language skills. Benefits of This Opportunity This Junior Professional position provides several career-development benefits, including: 1. Professional Growth Participants gain practical experience working on an international development programme while developing skills needed for careers in sustainability and global development. 2. Exposure to Global Projects The role provides experience working with: International development specialists Environmental professionals Investment teams Grant management experts Monitoring and evaluation specialists 3. Career Development Skills Participants can strengthen abilities in: Research Business analysis Project management Reporting Stakeholder engagement Sustainable investment planning 4. Contribution to Environmental Sustainability The opportunity allows professionals to contribute to projects focused on: Forest protection Climate resilience Sustainable agriculture Responsible investment Diversity, Inclusion, and Safeguarding Commitment Palladium promotes equity, diversity, and inclusion throughout its recruitment process. The organisation welcomes applications from people regardless of: Race Ethnicity Gender identity Religion Disability Nationality Socioeconomic background Age Other personal characteristics Palladium also provides reasonable adjustments during recruitment for candidates requiring accommodations. All successful candidates will participate in safeguarding-focused recruitment procedures and due diligence checks. Deadline and Application Information Application deadline: Not specified in the opportunity announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply early. APPLY HERE For more information about this opportunity, click here . Discover more Global Internship Opportunities here .
